bullets: Fourteen mainstream large language models audited for hiring discrimination using the paired-resume correspondence method of Kline, Rose, and Walters (2022), with 24,024 paired job postings run per model.

Each model screens matched resumes that vary race and gender and issues callback decisions; models are not individually named, and behavior is benchmarked against documented field-experiment discrimination gaps.

The one 2023-vintage model reproduces a pro-White gap of +2.12 pp at the 1% level, while every 2024-or-later model shows a null or pro-Black reversal up to -3.01 pp, with the gender axis mirroring this.
Thirty-six known past market episodes, including crises, bullish catalysts, and narrative reversals, used to test a market-narrative monitoring framework; sample period and geography not stated.
MACES runs four LLM agents (Bull, Bear, Skeptic, Editor) in adversarial discussion with ensemble scoring across Claude, GPT-4o, and Gemini, validated by detection rate against the 36 labeled events.
MACES reaches an 86% pre-event detection rate versus FinBERT's 67% and flags structural fragility in 10 events (28%) that FinBERT scores as neutral.
validation_note: 36 labeled market events; 86% pre-event detection vs FinBERT 67%

bullets: Methodological study on using LLM-generated features such as sentiment, classifications, and expectations as covariates in downstream regressions, with an application to news-based sentiment and stock returns.

Introduces AI-Powered Inference, a method-of-moments estimator combining bias correction from a small human-labeled calibration set, adaptive weighting over model-prompt pairs, and optimal calibration design; specific LLM not stated.
AI-PI yields conclusions stable across LLM and prompt choices where naive regressions vary substantially, with a confidence interval roughly half the length of using the human-labeled subset alone.
291,314 product launches posted to Product Hunt between January 2018 and April 2026, used to study whether generative AI narrows the diversity of startup ideas.
Uses ChatGPT's release as the shock and builds an outcome-blind LLM ideation-exposure index by category (index model not named) to measure convergence in launch concepts; no accuracy check reported.
After ChatGPT the share of distinctive launches falls from 25% to 16%, with larger similarity increases in higher-exposure categories, holding founder composition fixed.
Fifteen large language models from seven vendors evaluated on a 258-node vintage-correct macro panel of time-indexed financial decision tasks.

HindsightBench, a black-box audit protocol using a four-arm date-manipulation matrix, dual memory probes, and six per-model metrics, profiles parametric hindsight at probe-level cost; tested models include the Qwen lineage.

The date-trigger reflex tracks training generation not scale, absent in the 2024 open-weight 1B-70B models but present in all 2026 models; effective cutoffs span 22 months and precede vendor-reported dates by up to eight months.
Original 2026 survey of employees at a single two-year public college, with 50 valid respondents and an 18.9% response rate, on unauthorized workplace AI use.

No model is run by the researchers; the study documents employee use of consumer tools (ChatGPT at 72%, plus Gemini, Claude, and Copilot) and proposes a seven-dimension shadow-AI governance-gap framework.

Respondents report high self-assessed AI literacy (98%) and strong training demand (84%) yet prefer unsanctioned external tools, which the authors read as institutional enablement gaps rather than low risk awareness.
